objective: Expose all 11 supported CleanMac languages in the app language selection UI.
exit_condition: The goal is complete when CleanMac settings can present System plus all 11 AppLanguage options, every option has a localized display name, tests cover the 11-language language picker surface and resource coverage, all Localizable.strings files lint successfully, swift test passes, and the resulting changes are committed.

phases.name: Add coverage for all visible language options
Expose all supported languages in settings
Verify strings syntax and full SwiftPM test suite

current.focus: Goal complete: the HTML prototype exposes all 11 supported languages, automated tests guard the list, Swift tests and strings lint pass, and browser QA passed on desktop and mobile widths.
current.next_action: Stop scheduling after the commit succeeds.

decisions.decision: Use SwiftPM package resources with en.lproj and zh-Hans.lproj instead of adding L10nKit.
decisions.reason: CleanMac is a package-first macOS app with an existing lightweight L10n API; resource-backed lookup satisfies the requested .lproj migration without adding an unnecessary remote dependency.

decisions.decision: Keep AppLanguage storage values unchanged while mapping ResolvedLanguage to lproj folder names.
decisions.reason: Existing user defaults and tests expect english/chinese values, while Bundle resources need en and zh-Hans names.

decisions.decision: Try both zh-Hans and lowercase zh-hans when locating resource bundles.
decisions.reason: SwiftPM normalizes the built resource directory to zh-hans.lproj even though the source folder is zh-Hans.lproj.

decisions.decision: Do not stage the pre-existing untracked Assets directory.
decisions.reason: Those files are unrelated to the localization resource migration.

decisions.decision: Keep the 11-language ResolvedLanguage/AppLanguage model and focus this goal on the language selection surface.
decisions.reason: The current code already defines 11 resolved languages and has 11 .lproj resource folders, so the likely gap is the settings picker rather than the localization resource model.

decisions.decision: Update the HTML prototype language picker rather than Swift SettingsView.
decisions.reason: SettingsView already iterates AppLanguage.allCases, while the HTML prototype had a manually written language grid that had drifted from the 11-language model.

decisions.decision: Generate prototype language buttons from one LANGUAGES array and test that the array and I18N dictionaries both contain the 11 expected codes.
decisions.reason: A single source for the prototype picker plus an automated assertion prevents the UI from silently shrinking back to a smaller hardcoded list.

decisions.decision: Use script marks such as EN, 简, 繁, あ, and RU instead of flag emoji in the prototype language buttons.
decisions.reason: Browser QA showed some flag emoji rendering as missing glyphs, while script marks render reliably and still keep each option scannable.
